chapter: 1 Chesapeake Bay Water Quality Standards Attainment
heading: 1 Chesapeake Bay Water Quality Standards Attainment
text: Last updated: April 09, 2026Description: Chesapeake Bay Water Quality Attainment IndicatorIndicator family:Lower trophic levelsContributor(s): Qian Zhang, Richard Tian, Peter TangoAffiliations: University Maryland

chapter: 1 Chesapeake Bay Water Quality Standards Attainment
heading: 1.1 Introduction to Indicator
text: protect aquatic living resources Chesapeake Bay, Chesapeake Bay Program (CBP) partnership developed guidance framework ambient water quality criteria designated uses assessment procedures dissolved oxygen, chlorophyll-, water clarity/submerged aquatic vegetation (SAV) [2]. achieve consistent assessment time jurisdictions, multimetric indicator proposed CBP partnership provide means tracking progress 92 management segments Chesapeake Bay [3,4]. indicator computed three-year assessment period since 1985-1987, providing integrated measure Chesapeake Bay’s water quality condition last three decades.

chapter: 1 Chesapeake Bay Water Quality Standards Attainment
heading: 1.1.0.1 Data sources
text: multimetric indicator required monitoring data dissolved oxygen () concentrations, chlorophyll-concentrations, water clarity, SAV acreage, water temperature, salinity. SAV acreage measured Virginia Institute Marine Science collaboration CBP, available via http://web.vims.edu/bio/sav/StateSegmentAreaTable.htm. Data parameters obtained CBP Water Quality Database. data routinely reported CBP Maryland Department Natural Resources, Virginia Department Environmental Quality, Old Dominion University, Virginia Institute Marine Science, citizen/volunteer monitoring initiatives.

chapter: 1 Chesapeake Bay Water Quality Standards Attainment
heading: 1.1.0.2 Criteria attainment assessment
text: Monitoring data , chlorophyll-, water clarity/SAV processed compared water quality criteria thresholds according different designated uses (DUs). DUs migratory spawning nursery (MSN), open water (OW), deep water (DW), deep channel (DC), shallow water (SW), reflect seasonal nature water column structure life history needs living resources. Station-level chlorophyll-data spatially interpolated three dimensions. Salinity water temperature data used compute vertical density structure water column, translated layers different DUs. Criteria attainment determined comparing violation rates 3-year period reference cumulative frequency distribution represents extent allowable violation. approach implemented using FORTRAN codes, provided zipped folder. water clarity/SAV, single best year 3-year assessment period compared segment-specific acreage goal, water clarity goal, combination . details, refer Methods section Zhang et al. [5].

chapter: 1 Chesapeake Bay Water Quality Standards Attainment
heading: 1.2 Key Results and Visualizations

chapter: 1 Chesapeake Bay Water Quality Standards Attainment
heading: 1.2.0.1 Indicator calculation
text:
multimetric indicator quantifies fraction segment-DU-criterion combinations meet applicable season-specific thresholds 3-year assessment period 1985-1987 2021-2023. 3-year assessment period, applicable segment-DU-criterion combinations evaluated binomial fashion scored 1 “attainment” 0 “nonattainment”. classified status segment-DU-criterion combination weighted via segments’ surface area summed obtain multimetric index score. weighting scheme adopted two reasons: (1) segments vary size four orders magnitude, (2) surface area segment change time DUs, unlike seasonally variable habitat volume bottom water area [4]. details, refer Methods section Zhang et al. [5].indicator provides integrated measure Chesapeake Bay’s water quality condition (Figure 1). 2021-2023, 29.4% tidal water segment-DU-criterion combinations estimated met exceeded applicable water quality criteria thresholds.Mid-Atlantic
New England
code: #> [1] "This indicator is only present in the `MidAtlantic` report"

chapter: 1 Chesapeake Bay Water Quality Standards Attainment
heading: 1.3 Indicator statistics
text: Spatial scale: Chesapeake BayTemporal scale: 3-year assessment periods 1985-1987 2021-2023.Synthesis Theme:Multiple System Drivers

chapter: 1 Chesapeake Bay Water Quality Standards Attainment
heading: 1.4 Implications
text: Overall, indicator positive trend 0.19% per year 1985-1987 2021-2023, suggesting Chesapeake Bay recovery trajectory. Although p-value (0.16) meet 0.1 threshold statistical significance, may indicate weak trend largely impacted interannual variability streamflow.Patterns attainment variable among designated uses. Mann-Kendall trend analysis 1985–2023 (see Zhang et al. [5] methodology) revealed statistically significant long-term improvements deep water dissolved oxygen criterion attainment shallow water bay grasses/water clarity criterion attainment. improvements may indicate increasing resilience Bay ecosystem since restoration efforts began. Additional analyses conducted using non-binary metric attainment deficit. results revealed widespread long-term improvements dissolved oxygen attainment across Bay’s tidal waters, including statistically significant trends observed open water, deep water, four salinity zones (.e., tidal fresh, oligohaline, mesohaline, polyhaline). findings, consistent recent interpretations [6,7], provide evidence Bay’s increasing resilience following long-term nutrient management efforts.

heading: 3 Cetacean Passive Acoustic Monitoring
text: ConstructionLast updated: April 27, 2026Description: Passive acoustic monitoring (PAM) non-invasive method studying cetaceans, relying detection unique vocalizations cetaceans produce. PAM provides continuous sampling effort areas otherwise difficult access provides information multiple species simultaneously. resulting long-term, robust datasets allow insights species distributions, behavior, habitat use. Furthermore, PAM can used management perspective implement risk mitigation efforts, assess cumulative impacts stressors, evaluate potential behavioral distributional changes resulting climate change anthropogenic activity.Indicator family:MegafaunaContributor(s): Renea Briner, Amanda Holdman, Daryll Carlson, Genevieve Davis, Rhett Finley, Sophie Ferguson, Leila Hatch, Katie Kowarski, Bruce Martin, Jessica McCordic, Jennifer Miksis-Olds, Hilary Moors-Murphy, Xavier Mouy, Anita Murray, Maricarmen Serna, Erin Summers, Joy Stanistreet, Lindsey Transue, Christopher Tremblay, Annabel Westell, Rebecca Vanhoeck, Emma Vergow, Sofie Van Parijs, Mark Baumgartner, Joel Bell, Amy Engelhaupt, Paige Hanson, Hana Koilpillai, Melinda Rekdahl, Howard Rosenbaum, Julianne WilderAffiliations: NEFSC

heading: 3.1 Introduction to Indicator
text: Gulf Maine, examined weekly acoustic presence eight cetacean species (harbor porpoise sperm, humpback, minke, North Atlantic right, sei, fin, blue whales) one cetacean family (delphinid spp.). Data collected across four years (2019-2023) thirty recording sites. Mid-Atlantic Bight, examined weekly acoustic presence six baleen whale species (North Atlantic right, sei, humpback, fin, blue, minke whales). Data collected across two years (2022-2024) thirteen recording sites. summary allows improved understanding frequently species acoustically active whether exhibited seasonal patterns activity within region. data serves baseline summary previously understudied region historically viewed migratory corridor baleen species. full description preparation publication.

heading: 3.3 Indicator statistics
text: Spatial scale: total thirty PAM units located throughout Gulf Maine spanning coastal shelf, deep basins, offshore banks. total thirteen PAM units located throughout US Mid-Atlantic Bight shelf area Northwestern Atlantic Ocean. southernmost recorders located mouth Chesapeake Bay northernmost recorders located south Long Island, NY. Recorders arranged transects spanning continental shelf.Temporal scale: Gulf Maine, acoustic data summarized four-year continuous sampling period spanning September 2019 September 2023. Mid-Atlantic Bight, acoustic data summarized two-year continuous sampling period spanning June 2022 November 2024.Synthesis Theme:Multiple System Drivers

heading: 3.4 Implications
text:
Acoustic summaries show Gulf Maine Mid-Atlantic Bight important habitats cetaceans, many species detected year-round. Peak acoustic presence varied species/family, groups exhibiting seasonal presence patterns. fine-scale analyses reveal interannual subregional differences cetacean acoustic presence, highlighting dynamic nature region.Gulf MaineContinuous Presence: Harbor porpoises, delphinids, humpback whales, fin whales acoustically present days week year-round, slight decrease harbor porpoise fin whale detections summer (May-July) slight decrease humpback whale detections late-winter (February) summer (June-August).
Winter Presence: Blue whales detected weeks throughout fall winter (September-February).Summer Absence: Sei whales detected throughout fall, winter, spring, fewer days detections summer (June-August). Sei whale acoustic presence particularly peaked spring (March-May) fall (October-November). North Atlantic right whale acoustic presence followed similar trends sei whale acoustic presence, least days detections summer, days detections throughout fall, winter, spring. However, right whales fewer detections sei whales throughout fall early-winter, right whale acoustic presence peaked earlier spring (March) sei whale acoustic presence.Highly Variable: Minke whale acoustic presence greatly varied, generally consistent days week detected throughout spring, summer, fall (March-November). Sperm whales detected year-round, highly variable number days detections week. Variability likely driven interannual subregional differences, indicator covers multiple habitat types.Mid-Atlantic BightWinter Presence: Humpback, North Atlantic right, fin whales detected weeks throughout winter (November-February). Leading winter months, fall spring acoustic presence varied three species, North Atlantic right whales shortest peak detection window (December-January). Blue whales also acoustically present occasions winter.
Spring Presence: Sei whales showed peak detections spring (April-May) sporadic acoustic presence months.Summer Presence: species consistently detected summer months (June-July); however, humpback, minke, North Atlantic right, sei, fin whales detected least .Highly Variable: Minke whale acoustic presence greatly varied site year, generally peaked spring (March-April) late summer fall (August-October).

chapter: 4 Ambient Sounds Levels in the NE
heading: 4 Ambient Sounds Levels in the NE
text: ConstructionLast updated: April 27, 2026Description: Passive acoustic monitoring (PAM) simultaneously captures biological, anthropogenic, climatological, geological sounds occurring area. PAM provides continuous sampling effort areas otherwise difficult access, resulting long-term, robust datasets. Ambient sound (soundscape) metrics show distribution acoustic energy across frequencies. Peaks sound levels within particular frequency bins indicate louder sounds frequencies, frequencies 1000 Hz typically representing contributions baleen whales, vessel traffic, noise-generating fishes. Therefore, soundscapes can used indicator ecosystem health identify changes sound sources, indicative changes phenology, species distributions, storm activity, human activity.Indicator family:OceanographicOceanographicHabitatHabitatContributor(s): Renea Briner, Amanda Holdman, Genevieve Davis, Rhett Finley, Sophie Ferguson, Leila Hatch, Samara Haver, Jessica McCordic, Xavier Mouy, Anita Murray, Maricarmen Serna, Anne Smith, Erin Summers, Lindsey Transue, Christopher Tremblay, Annabel Westell, Rebecca Vanhoeck, Sofie Van Parijs, Isabella Garfield, Joel Bell, Amy EngelhauptAffiliations: NEFSC

chapter: 4 Ambient Sounds Levels in the NE
heading: 4.1 Introduction to Indicator
text: indicator examines hybrid millidecade sound pressure levels. Gulf Maine, data collected across four half years (2020-2024) nine recording sites. Mid-Atlantic Bight, data collected across two years (2022-2024) nine recording sites. metrics allow improved understanding dominant sound sources whether sound sources exhibited spatial seasonal patterns activity within region. full description preparation publication.

heading: 4.2 Key Results and Visualizations

chapter: 4 Ambient Sounds Levels in the NE
heading: 4.3 Indicator statistics
text: Spatial scale: Hybrid millidecade metrics provided nine sites throughout Gulf Maine. Sites broadly cluster three subregions: coastal Gulf Maine (Lubec, MDR, Monhegan, Portland, York), Stellwagen Bank National Marine Sanctuary (SB03), edge George’s Bank offshore Gulf Maine (USTR01, USTR03, USTR11). Hybrid millidecade metrics provided nine sites throughout Mid-Atlantic Bight. southernmost recorders located mouth Chesapeake Bay northernmost recorders located mouth Delaware Bay. Recorders arranged transects spanning continental shelf, inshore sites ending “01” offshore sites “03”.Temporal scale: Gulf Maine, acoustic data summarized four---half-year continuous sampling period spanning January 2020 July 2024. Mid-Atlantic Bight, acoustic data summarized two-year continuous sampling period spanning June 2022 November 2024.Synthesis Theme:Multiple System Drivers

chapter: 4 Ambient Sounds Levels in the NE
heading: 4.4 Implications
text: Gulf MaineAmbient sound metrics show Gulf Maine dynamic region dominant sound sources varying spatially temporally. coastal sites, like Lubec, tidal flow noise strongly influences year-round soundscape, demonstrated repetitive patterns increased sound 40 Hz coincide semidiurnal tidal cycle. sites within Stellwagen Bank National Marine Sanctuary offshore Gulf Maine, fin whale vocalizations seasonally contribute soundscape, demonstrated peaks around 20 Hz throughout fall spring (September-March).Mid-Atlantic BightAmbient sound metrics show Mid-Atlantic Bight important habitat numerous soniferous fish populations, dominant sound source region fish choruses. contributions fish choruses varied spatially temporally, highest chorus sound levels recorded three inshore sites (.e. DB01, ES01, CB01) throughout summer fall, least three choruses observed. chorus peaked distinct octave level (OL) bands, occupying 500, 1000, 2000 Hz OLs significantly.

heading: 5 Bottom Temperature - in situ
text: Last updated: November 13, 2024Description: data presented time series regional average bottom temperature anomalies ship-based measurements made Northeast Continental Shelf.Indicator family:OceanographicContributor(s): Paula Fratantoni; Chris Melrose; Tamara Holzwarth-DavisAffiliations: NEFSC

heading: 5.1 Introduction to Indicator
text: bottom temperature index incorporates near-bottom temperature measurements collected Northeast Fisheries Science Center (NEFSC) surveys 1977-present. Early measurements made using surface bucket samples, mechanical bathythermographs expendable bathythermograph probes, 1991 CTD – acronym conductivity temperature depth – became standard equipment NEFSC surveys. Near-bottom refers deepest observation station falls within 10 m reported water depth. Observations encompass entire continental shelf area extending Cape Hatteras, NC Nova Scotia, Canada, inclusive Gulf Maine Georges Bank.

heading: 7 Ecosystem overfishing
text: DevelopmentLast updated: April 27, 2026Description: Ecosystem overfishing ecological, legal, term ultimately evaluates much fish caught ecosystem relative much can produced. Several indices used evaluate ecosystem overfishing, Ryther index, Fogarty index, primary production required.Indicator family:MegafaunaMegafaunaEconomicEconomicContributor(s): Andrew BeetAffiliations: NEFSC

heading: 7.1 Introduction to Indicator
text: Ryther index defined total catch per unit area ecosystem [8]. units mt km^-2 year^-1. measures total removal fish biomass area Large Marine Ecosystem relative much entire ecosystem can produce. general terms, lower Ryther index, less likely ecosystem experiencing ecosystem overfishing.Fogarty index defined ratio total catches total primary productivity ecosystem [8]. units parts per thousand.modification indices used. Total landings used lieu total catch. Accountng total IUU (Illegal, unreported, unregulated fishing) data, spatial footprint required, currently available. effect reducing value index (compared using total catch, including IUU).

Additionally, warm core rings interact shelf break, can create biological hotspots. El Niño winters, noted increased frequency East Coast storms. heading: 11.2 Key Results and Visualizations
text: Northeast U.S. region experienced colder average ocean temperatures, despite record warm global ocean air temperatures. Similar 2024, oceanographic ecological conditions 2025 reflected cooler water changing species abundance, distribution, timing.

heading: 11.2.0.1 Global
text: According 2025 NOAA NCEI Global Temperature Analysis, global upper ocean (0-700 meters) heat content record high 2025, surpassing previous record set 2024. Ocean heat content key climate indicator oceans store 90% excess heat Earth system. 0–700 m global ocean heat content, tracked globally since 1955, experienced steady upward trend since 1970. 2025 fifth consecutive year OHC experienced highest value. Additional notable global annual highlights include:2025 third-warmest year NOAA global temperature record, dates back 1850.Annual sea ice extent Arctic Antarctic regions ranked among three lowest years record.Northern Hemisphere snow cover extent third lowest record.101 named tropical storms across globe 2025, average.

heading: 11.2.0.2 Northwest Atlantic Phenomena
text: average temperatures observed 2024 persisted 2025, however, near-normal salinities suggest shift underlying oceanographic drivers compared low-salinity signal 2024. Surface temperatures anomalously cold throughout region winter, slightly average spring early summer, mostly average August December, although local exceptions pattern. Offshore shelf-break Gulf Stream, surface temperatures average much year.Winter bottom temperatures also average across much Northeast Shelf. average March May temperatures southern Mid-Atlantic likely attributed northward moving Gulf Stream water entered shelf near Cape Hatteras. average May June temperatures eastern edge Georges Bank associated Warm Core Ring. mechanisms driving August October average bottom temperatures Mid-Atlantic Bight currently unknown.Multiple large scale oceanographic atmospheric factors can contribute cooler conditions including position Gulf Stream proportions Labrador Slope Scotian Shelf water entering system (Gonçalves Neto et al. 2021). Gulf Stream position 2024 2025 southernly since 2007 relatively flat (.e. fewer meanders) -76o -64o West. Warm core rings form Gulf Stream meanders. 2025 ~25 warm core rings majority formed east 65oW.Hurricane Erin one notable storms 2025 caused significant shoreside oceanographic disturbances despite making landfall Northeast (National Climate Report). August 16th, Erin underwent rapid intensification, central pressure dropping 78 millibars 24 hours, third largest pressure drop record Atlantic basin. Although Erin remained offshore continental United States, expansive wind field large size resulted widespread impacts. Along Northeast coastline, Erin generated waves 20 feet (6 m) along Outer Banks North Carolina August 19th 22th. highest wind gusts coastal parts New Jersey, Delaware, Maryland ranged 40 50 mph. Many beaches Maryland Maine closed due rough surf, large waves, rip currents, coastal flooding led road closures several water rescues, particularly New Jersey. Beach erosion significant locations. Hurricane Erin’s strong winds large size caused mixing weakened stratification throughout Mid-Atlantic resulting cooler average surface waters across shelf Slope Sea.

heading: 11.2.0.3 Northeast Shelf
text: marine heatwaves observed either Georges Bank Gulf Maine 2025 one short lived (7 days) heatwave observed Mid-Atlantic. contrast, several marine cold spells, extended periods extreme colder average temperatures, occurred 2025.Five surface marine cold spells occurred Gulf Maine 2025. fifth strongest surface cold spell record started February 6th lasted 42 days. average sea surface temperature 4.50 oC, almost 1 oC cooler 2016-2025 Gulf Maine average surface temperature (5.47 oC) time period (February 6 March 20). Three bottom marine cold spells occurred Gulf Maine 2025. 5th strongest record started December 24, 2024, peaked January 13, 2025, lasted 83 days. average bottom temperature Gulf Maine 7.5 oC, 1 oC cooler average Gulf Maine bottom temperature (8.6 oC) time period (December 24 March 17).Three surface marine cold spells occurred Georges Bank, notable November, ranked 11th strongest record. 9th strongest bottom cold spell started August 11th, peaked Sep 15th, likely continued throughout fall (final analyses still pending).Six surface marine cold spells occurred Mid-Atlantic Bight 2025, notable occurred February. 8th strongest record lasted 31 days. One bottom cold spell occurred Mid-Atlantic January. 5th strongest record lasted 57 days. average bottom temperature cold spell 7.2 oC, almost 2 oC cooler climatological average bottom temperature (9.0 oC) time-period (January 9th March 7th).cooler bottom water temperatures contributed well established Mid-Atlantic Cold Pool 2024 2025. spatial extent Cold Pool average, duration mean temperature near long-term mean. deviates long-term trends smaller, shorter warm annual cold pool indices.2025 total primary production average Georges Bank Mid-Atlantic due lower phytoplankton biomass cooler sea surface temperatures. Phytoplankton biomass (shown chlorophyll concentration) also average much 2025. particular, winter-spring bloom period, typically accounts significant proportion total annual phytoplankton production, shorter duration lower magnitude across entire Northeast shelf region. fall bloom period average Gulf Maine Georges Bank, near average Mid-Atlantic.shift cooler waters 2024-2025 likely linked multiple observations across Northeast Shelf including uncommon presence Arctic zooplankton species Gulf Maine, delayed migration many species, redistribution species. shifts affect availability species surveys fishing, although aggregate species distributions cooler 2024-2025 period tracking long-term trend towards northward deeper waters.

text: Chesapeake Bay, colder average winter 2025 temperatures reported state agencies likely cause higher blue crab mortality rates compared previous winter. Colder winters generally indicative good conditions striped bass spawning slight improvement striped bass juvenile index, still well long-term average. Several years low striped bass recruitment growing concern fisheries managers. Factors influencing striped bass include lower average freshwater flow late winter early spring combination higher average water temperatures lower dissolved oxygen values optimal survival thresholds summer. Chesapeake Bay management concerns include continued presence invasive blue catfish effect blue crab, alosines, menhaden, striped bass populations.Mid-Atlantic scallops Elephant Trunk region showing positive signs following documented die-. Two-year olds observed 2024 good survival 2025 survey. Elephant Trunk region scheduled reopen 2026. also good survival 2024 recruits southeastern Nantucket Lightship Area 2025. contrast, large numbers scallop predator Asterias vulgaris sea stars linked increased sea scallop mortality 2024 2025 Georges Bank.

Scientists working charter captains Chesapeake Bay reported low catch rates striped bass red drum June-September, higher catch rates early spring fall.Fishers attributed delayed migration black sea bass inshore, scup migrating south winter using similar routes early 1970s due cooler temperatures.Members bluefish fishery Rhode Island reported low landings 2025 attributed changes seasonal migration path timing.species, Atlantic mackerel, Illex squid sandlance, observed higher abundance wider distributions compared recent years.Fishers Gulf Maine Georges Bank mixed reports lobsters good catch sea scallops.Fishers reported fewer warm water species 2025 along New Jersey coast. Others, however, noted new species (e.g., pomapano, spadefish, triggerfish, Spanish king mackerel) Delaware Bay.Anglers also observed low spring summer catches gamefish Mid-Atlantic bays shelf, high concentrations shark species near coast.

heading: 11.2.0.6 Offshore Wind
text: Active offshore wind projects continue developed throughout region. Southern New England, South Fork Wind Farm remains first commercial scale project operation (12 turbines). Vineyard Wind 1 Revolution Wind continued construction, Empire Wind 1 New York Bight Sunrise Wind began offshore construction 2025. Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind (CVOW) also continued construction Mid-Atlantic. projects currently construction anticipated complete end 2026. New London, CT New Bedford, MA expanded dedicated space infrastructure offshore wind industry increased port activity first projects construction southern New England. eight additional projects Construction Operations Plan (COP) approvals (three Southern New England five Mid-Atlantic/New York Bight) begin construction 2026. However, construction schedules highly uncertain time.

chapter: 13 State of the Ecosystem Plain Language Glossary
heading: 13 State of the Ecosystem Plain Language Glossary
text:
Apex Predator:
Predators natural predators , large sharks, toothed whales, seals, tunas, billfish.Benthivore:
Predator feeding bottom-dwelling prey, lobster haddock.Benthos:
Organisms live sea bottom ([13]), scallop quahog.Bmsy:
weight (biomass) group fish necessary produce maximum sustainable yield (MSY) ([14]).Catch:
total number (weight) fish caught fishing operations. component fish comes contact fishing gear, retained gear ([15]).Climate Vulnerability:
degree habitat/species unable cope negative impacts climate change.Climatology:
Average conditions specific time period.Cold Pool:
Area relatively cold bottom water forms US northeast shelf Mid-Atlantic Bight.Commercial Fishery:
Large-scale industry selling fish, shellfish aquatic animals.Community Engagement:
mathematical measure engaged community commercial fisheries. index includes amount landings, dealers permits.Conceptual Model:
representation current understanding major system features processes particular environment ([13]).Condition:
mathematical measurement “plumpness,” general health fish group fishes ([16]).Continental Shelf:
Underwater portion (shelf) continent, extending seaward shore edge continental slope depth increases rapidly ([15]).Continental Slope:
Part continental margin; ocean floor continental shelf continental rise ([13]).Ecological Production Unit (EPU):
specific geographic region similar physical features plankton characteristics supporting ecological community within large marine ecosystem (LME).Ecosystem Assessment:
social process findings science concerning causes ecosystem change, consequences human well-, management policy options presented decision makers ([15]).Effort:
amount time fishing power used harvest fish; includes gear size, boat size, horsepower ([16]).Elasmobranch:
Describes group fish without hard bony skeleton, including sharks, skates, rays ([15]).Endangered Species:
species defined US Endangered Species Act, danger extinction significant portion range ([17]).Energy Density:
measurement amount energy (calories) contained certain amount food prey organism.Estuary:
Coastal body brackish water may important nursery habitat many species interest.Estuarine:
Conditions found estuary: shallow water, high variability water temperature, salt content, nutrients, oxygen level.Eutrophication:
enrichment water nutrients causing increased growth algae higher forms plant life creating imbalance organisms present water quality water live ([18]).Exclusive Economic Zone:
EEZ area extends seaward boundaries coastal states 3 200 nautical miles U.S. coast. Within area, United States claims exclusive fishery management authority fishery resources ([19]).Feeding Guild:
group species consuming similar prey species; example, planktivores different species eat plankton.Fishery:
combination fish fishers region, latter fishing similar species similar gear types ([13]).Fishery-Dependent Data:
Data collected directly fish fishery commercial sport fishermen seafood dealers. Common methods include logbooks, trip tickets, port sampling, fishery observers, phone surveys ([16]).Fishery-Independent Data:
Stock/habitat/environmental data collected independently activity fishing sector usually research vessel ([15]).Fmsy:
rate removal fish population fishing , applied constantly, result maximum sustainable yield (MSY) ([15]).Forage Species:
Species used prey larger predator food. Includes small schooling fishes anchovies, sardines, herrings, capelin, smelts, menhaden ([15]).GB:
George’s Bank Ecological Production Unit ([20]).GOM:
Gulf Maine Ecological Production Unit ([20]).Groundfish:
Group commercially harvested ocean bottom-oriented fish cooler regions Northern Hemisphere including cods, flounders, associated species. exact species list varies regionally.Gulf Stream:
warm ocean current flowing northward along eastern United States.Habitat:
1. environment fish live, including everything surrounds affects life, e.g. water quality, bottom, vegetation, associated species (including food supplies); 2. site particular type local environment occupied organism ([15]).Harvest:
total number weight fish caught kept area period time ([16]).Highly Migratory Species:
Marine species whose life cycle includes lengthy migrations, usually exclusive economic zones two countries well international waters. term usually used denote tuna tuna-like species, sharks, swordfish, billfish ([15]).Ichthyoplankton:
Fish eggs larvae belonging planktonic community ([15]).Indicator:
1. variable, pointer, index. fluctuation reveals variations key elements system. position trend indicator relation reference points values indicate present state dynamics system. Indicators provide bridge objectives action ([15]).Landings:
